mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in >55% of triggered instances over 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median returns over 60 trading days in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above trend during late-cycle expansions reflects resilient manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above trend coincides with negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized confirms sustained economic expansion, which directly supports corporate profit growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2% yields negative median 60-day returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above threshold produce positive returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer income expectations and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns in >55% of observations over 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts during late-cycle expansions signals weakening real estate investment and broader demand contraction, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines coincide with positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances.
